channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The War on Spam Calls: How to Reduce Unwanted Calls
item.description: Learn how to reduce unwanted calls by adding yourself to the FTC's Do Not Call list, using call-blocking features and apps, protecting your personal info online, and not engaging with suspicious callers.
item.dc:creator: Doug Aamoth, Fast Company

item.title: TikTok’s ‘recession brunette’ trend signals tough economic times
item.description: Noticed all the blondes going back to their natural hair color lately? As much as many try to claim it’s because of a “hair health journey,” other factors seem to be at play here. “How’s the economy?” one TikTok user asks in a viral video.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: The adorable BDX Droids are coming to more Disney Parks around the world, including Disney World
item.description: Disney Parks is bringing the adorable and impressive 'BDX Droids' to more parks worldwide, including Disney World. Plus, more details were unveiled for the update on the Smugglers Run attraction at Galaxy's Edge.
item.dc:creator: jacob.krol@futurenet.com (Jacob Krol), TechRadar

item.title: Alibaba's DAMO Academy Launches Server-Grade RISC-V Processor: A Game-Changer in High-Performance Computing?
item.description: Chinese tech giant Alibaba has launched its first server-grade RISC-V processor, the C930, designed for high-performance computing and positioning it as an alternative to Intel Xeon and AMD EPYC CPUs.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: At the tactical edge, Army wants 58 zero trust capabilities
item.description: CWO Four Ben Koontz, the director of the Army’s functional management office for zero trust, said a recent pilot successfully tested these cyber tools. The post At the tactical edge, Army wants 58 zero trust capabilities first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Trump hosts crypto leaders at White House summit
item.description: The cryptocurrency industry flocked to Washington on Friday for the White House’s first-ever crypto summit, where President Trump and his Cabinet made clear the “war on crypto” is over in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: i2CAT promueve el primer laboratorio abierto europeo de investigación 6G en órbita baja
item.description: El centro de investigación i2CAT ha anunciado en el marco del Mobile World Congress la puesta en marcha del primer laboratorio en órbita terrestre baja de Europa (LEO, por sus siglas en inglés).
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Microsoft 365: A guide to the updates
item.description: Microsoft 365 (and Office 365) subscribers get more frequent software updates than those who have purchased Office without a subscription, which means subscribers have access to the latest features, security patches, and bug fixes.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: When to choose a bare-metal cloud
item.description: I did something last week that I had not done for a while. I recommended a bare-metal cloud service to a client. They had a particular purpose, and it was the right technology to solve the problem.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Siete referentes de liderazgo femenino en TI para el 8M
item.description: Pese a los importantes avances en materia de igualdad entre hombres y mujeres que se han logrado en los últimos años, el de la equidad es un camino al que aún le queda recorrido. También en el sector tecnológico, en el que aún persiste cierta segregación por género.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Gig Companies are backing Trump’s Labor Secretary nominee. Here’s what that means for workers
item.description: The trade association representing America’s largest gig companies is backing President Trump’s nominees to lead the Department of Labor—an endorsement that could shape the future of worker classification in the gig economy.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Alibaba says its new AI model rivals DeepSeeks’s R-1, OpenAI’s o1
item.description: Alibaba Cloud on Thursday launched QwQ-32B, a compact reasoning model built on its latest large language model (LLM), Qwen2.5-32b, one it says delivers performance comparable to other large cutting edge models, including Chinese rival DeepSeek and OpenAI’s o1, with only 32 billion parameters.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Trump signs executive order establishing bitcoin reserve
item.description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order Thursday establishing a government reserve of bitcoin, a key marker in the cryptocurrency's journey towards possible mainstream acceptance. Under Trump's new order, the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ALAN SUDERMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Albania shuts down TikTok for a year amid concerns over violence among children
item.description: TIRANA, Albania (AP) — The Albanian Cabinet decided on Thursday to shut down TikTok for 12 months, blaming the popular video-sharing platform for inciting violence and bullying, especially among children.
item.dc:creator: LLAZAR SEMINI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Elon Musk’s Starlink could soon tap into $42 billion federal program
item.description: The Commerce Department has changed its rules in a way that could open the door for Starlink, Elon Musk’s satellite internet service, to become part of a federal $42 billion grant program to bring high-speed service to rural and poor areas of the United States.
item.dc:creator: CNN.com Wire Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Siemens announces $285 million investment in US manufacturing
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Siemens plans to invest $285 million in manufacturing in the United States, the tech company said Thursday, including two new facilities in California and Texas. The U.S. is the largest market for the company. The recent investments in the Siemens' U.S.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Chrome Enterprise moves to better differentiate work, personal browsing
item.description: Google on Thursday unveiled new security controls for Chrome Enterprise, allowing IT departments to help employees better distinguish between work and personal browsing profiles.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Trump says he will 'probably' extend TikTok ban deadline if deal isn't reached in time
item.description: President Trump on Thursday suggested he would consider extending the deadline for a ban on TikTok for a second time if a deal isn't reached, stating there is "a lot of interest" in the video-sharing platform.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Utah becomes the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify ages
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Utah on Wednesday became the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify users’ ages and get parental consent for minors to download apps to their devices. The bill headed to the desk of Gov.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Google experiments with AI-only search as competition heats up
item.description: Google has launched an experimental search mode that replaces traditional links with AI-generated summaries, as it ramps up efforts to integrate AI amid growing competition. Currently, Google Search displays AI Overviews, which provide summaries above traditional search results.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to handle type erasure in advanced Java generics
item.description: Generics programming in Java enhances type safety and code reusability by allowing developers to define classes and methods using type parameters.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: C++ Memory Safety: Bjarne Stroustrup Promotes Profiles for Safety
item.description: C++ memory safety issues have come under heat recently, but C++ designer Bjarne Stroustrup remains steadfast in promoting profiles as essential for safety in C++.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AI pioneers who channeled 'hedonistic' machines win computer science's top prize
item.description: Teaching machines in the way that animal trainers mold the behavior of dogs or horses has been an important method for developing artificial intelligence and one that was recognized Wednesday with the top computer science award.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Reddit co-founder Alexis Ohanian joins bid to acquire TikTok
item.description: Reddit co-founder Alexis Ohanian has joined billionaire Frank McCourt’s bid to acquire TikTok as a strategic adviser.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Minnesota considers blocking 'nudify' apps that use AI to make explicit images without consent
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — Molly Kelley was stunned to discover in June that someone she knew had used widely available “nudification” technology to create highly realistic and sexually explicit videos and images of her, using family photos that were posted on social media.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: AirPods dying? Professor discovers microenvironment that may be responsible
item.description: UT-Austin associate professor Yijin Lu said a similar microenvironment exists in all sorts of electronics. This includes places like your phone and electric vehicles.
item.dc:creator: Eric Henrikson, NewsNation

item.title: Startup Fractile tackles AI inference bottlenecks with new chip design
item.description: A 29-year-old entrepreneur with a Ph.D in robotics is looking to shake up the AI chip industry with an innovative approach that promises to deliver hardware that is 100 times faster, 10 times cheaper, and 20 times more energy efficient than the Nvidia GPUs that dominate the market today.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 2025 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World
